title: Graph degradation semantics hardening
goal: Audit and harden graph-related outputs so stale, missing, invalid, degraded or unavailable graph evidence is reported consistently and cannot be read as reachability, causality, impact completeness or default-promotion readiness.

acceptance.assertion: Graph consumers use a consistent degradation vocabulary across docs, CLI, availability and retrieval-adjacent outputs.
Outputs explicitly avoid causality, runtime reachability, completeness and impact claims.
Affected output paths have tests or a precise coverage gap is recorded.

metadata.outcome: Implemented in Lenskit PR #924: graph load, graph availability, query explain diagnostics, context compiler gaps and delta-context graph gaps now share a diagnostic degradation vocabulary. Only ok/available graph evidence is retrieval-eligible; stale, missing, invalid, degraded or profile-excluded graph evidence is explicitly non-promotional and does not establish runtime reachability, causality, change impact, graph completeness or default-promotion readiness.
validation: Lenskit PR #924 GitHub CI passed: pytest-full, ruff, CodeQL, doc-freshness, planning-registration, validate-merge-meta, contracts, graph checks, ai-context guards and webui-js-tests.
Before merge: direct self-review found and fixed generic graph_degradation_report ok-status consistency; added direct test coverage.
Before merge local: docs/tasks/index.json parsed, planning-registration ratchet had 0 new findings, git diff --check passed, 70 focused tests passed and targeted ruff passed.
Reviews/inline comments were empty; Codex did not produce a code review because the account had reached usage limits.
Post-merge on Lenskit origin/main 2cacb7bf7f29f7692007f45cebf2ab7b97220e17: same 70 focused tests passed, targeted ruff passed, planning-registration ratchet passed and task index JSON parsed.

verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t010-vocabulary: Shared graph_degradation.py vocabulary is consumed by snapshot availability, query explain diagnostics, context compiler graph gaps and delta-context graph gaps.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t010-no-causality: Graph degradation reports and gaps explicitly do not establish runtime reachability, runtime causality, change impact, graph completeness, retrieval improvement, default-promotion readiness or merge readiness.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t010-tests: Added direct graph degradation semantics tests and strengthened graph availability/retrieval tests; post-merge focused suite passed 70 tests.
